import torch
from model import create_model

ckpt = torch.load('checkpoints/best_model_clip.pt', map_location='cpu')
print('Checkpoint keys (first 10):')
for i,k in enumerate(ckpt.keys()):
    print(k)
    if i>=9: break

model = create_model(num_classes=345, backbone='clip')
print('\nModel keys (first 10):')
for i,k in enumerate(model.state_dict().keys()):
    print(k)
    if i>=9: break
